Section 01

Voices of Our Team

Meet the graduate students and faculty who make GSC happen — their stories, motivations, and what this community means to them personally.

Faculty Mentor · Graduate Student Community

Farrokh Mistree

The Graduate Student Community continues to energize and uplift engineering students across OU, especially those in Aerospace and Mechanical Engineering. Today's graduate students are pushing boundaries in research, leadership, and innovation. They thrive best when surrounded by a strong, connected community. That is where the GSC makes a real difference. With your support as AME alumni and friends of the Gallogly College of Engineering, we can expand mentoring networks, host high-impact events, and create spaces where future engineers feel inspired and supported. Your contribution keeps this momentum alive and strengthens the community that shaped so many of us.

Samuel Espinoza

GSC means collective success to me. When I started my graduate program, I was not aware of any resources or groups I could become involved in. That changed when I received an email invitation from the GSC. Being part of the leadership team has helped me grow both professionally and personally.

Roshan Sameer Annam

Graduate school a lot of times can feel lonely and daunting. GSC actively works towards fostering a community where everyone feels they belong and that their voice matters. Being part of the leadership team opens you up to a world of opportunities — leadership, mentorship, and career avenues through a strong alumni network.

Siavash Mandegari

GSC means a supportive family where graduate students can connect, share ideas, and learn from one another. It represents a community that encourages collaboration, leadership, and professional growth. I have learned the importance of teamwork, initiative, and finding joy while managing academic responsibilities.

Anshu Raj

GSC means engagement and shared learning to me. It brings together graduate students willing to exchange ideas, listen to diverse perspectives, and work to improve the student experience. Through these interactions, I continue to learn and help build a supportive graduate environment.

Avinash Nayal

GSC means community, belonging, and growth to me. Graduate school can be demanding, and GSC creates a space where students can connect, feel included, and grow beyond their academic work. It has given me the opportunity to help build community through events and initiatives that bring people together.

Swapneel Danayat

GSC for me means an ocean of opportunities. I have been part of this wonderful team for five years, and my journey as a graduate student would have been incomplete without it. GSC helped me develop skills that complement my academic growth while contributing meaningfully to the graduate community.

Hafiz Ahmad Hassan

GSC means connection, learning, and growth to me. One of the most impactful parts of my experience has been the GSC webinars, which introduced me to ideas beyond my own research area. Through LinkedIn promotions, I have helped increase awareness and connect more students with broader opportunities.

Zarin Tasnim

GSC means stepping beyond the routine of graduate research and engaging with a broader community of ideas and people. Through GSC I've had the chance to listen to new perspectives in webinars, help plan activities, and work with others to bring ideas to life that benefit all of us as we grow together.

Vibha Karnail

GSC means community, opportunity, and support to me. Through webinars and knowledge-sharing events on emerging topics — like our focus on AI — we help create opportunities for students to learn about new technological advancements and stay informed about the future of their fields.

Suramyaa Sarkar

GSC means collaboration, curiosity, and shared growth to me. Organizing events, discussions, and outreach activities helps create spaces where graduate students feel encouraged to share ideas and build professional relationships. GSC is where collaboration, mentorship, and curiosity come together to strengthen the graduate experience at OU.

Section 02

Events & Initiatives

GSC builds real community through a mix of academic events, social gatherings, and student-led initiatives that connect graduate students across the college.

01

Weekly Webinar Series

One of GSC's flagship initiatives is the weekly seminar series, typically hosted every Wednesday or Thursday during the semester. These sessions give graduate students the opportunity to hear from faculty members, industry professionals, and researchers presenting cutting-edge work.

The seminars serve as a platform for discussing emerging research topics, sharing technical insights, and exploring career pathways in both academia and industry. Attendance typically ranges from 20 to 40 participants per session.

AcademicWeeklyFree on YouTube
02

3MT & Professional Development

GSC mentors and supports students who participate in the Three Minute Thesis (3MT) competition, helping them distill complex research into a clear, engaging presentation. GSC also supports students in events such as ASME Central Oklahoma Chapter Lightning Talks.

These programs help students sharpen their research communication skills and build confidence presenting to diverse audiences inside and outside academia.

3MTASMEProfessional Skills
03

Graduate Student Handbook

Originally written by graduate students for graduate students, the GSC Graduate Handbook guides new students through graduate life at OU. It covers thesis and dissertation timelines, finding advisors, housing, campus resources, and scholarship opportunities.

The handbook has evolved into a freely accessible online resource, updated regularly — a lasting contribution to every incoming cohort.

Free ResourceNew StudentsOnline
04

Community & Social Events

GSC organizes the Faculty, Staff and Student Mixer — formerly an international food festival — celebrating the diverse backgrounds of the graduate community. Participants share food, cultural experiences, and conversations that build lasting connections.

Other events include game nights, potluck gatherings, leadership team outings, and meet-and-greet events at the start of each semester to help incoming students integrate into the community.

Food FestivalGame NightsCommunity Building

GSC by Impact

  • Weekly research seminars open to all graduate students
  • 3MT and ASME Lightning Talk coaching for student presenters
  • Graduate Handbook — free, student-written, always online
  • International food festival celebrating cultural diversity
  • Game nights, potlucks, and social outings every semester
  • Meet & Greet events for every incoming cohort

Section 03

Explore Some of Our Latest Webinars

Our webinar series invites faculty and industry professionals to share how they use Generative AI in research, work, and entrepreneurial settings. All recordings are free on YouTube.

Speakers have joined us from OU, Oklahoma State University, Purdue University, IIT Delhi, IISc Bengaluru, and industry ventures in the US and UAE — giving students a genuinely global perspective on where AI is headed.

Talks are organized into three thematic clusters: AI in Learning, Work & Careers; Agentic AI, Information Systems & Ethics; and Generative AI for Domain Science & Engineering Design.

1

Cluster 01Generative AI, Learning, Work & Careers

GenAI as an Enabler of Learning
Vistasp M. Karbhari
Professor of Civil, Mechanical & Aerospace Engineering · UT Arlington
▶ Watch

Vistasp invites participants to rethink how learners at every stage of life pair their judgment with generative AI to deepen understanding rather than shortcut it.

Will AI Make or Break Engineering Careers?
Haotian Liu & Dan Fisher
Oklahoma State University
▶ Watch

Haotian and Dan invite early-career engineers to ask which parts of engineering practice remain distinctly human when AI tools generate reports, analyze data, and create preliminary designs.

A Work Psychology View of AI's Potential
Eric Day
Professor of Industrial & Organizational Psychology · University of Oklahoma
▶ Watch

Eric invites participants to look at GenAI from the perspective of people at work — how AI changes hiring, performance, wellbeing, and fairness in real organizations.

2

Cluster 02Agentic AI, Information Systems & Ethics

Are GenAI and LLMs Reshaping Human-Computer Dynamics?
Vishal Singh
Associate Professor · Indian Institute of Science (IISc), Bengaluru
▶ Watch

Vishal invites participants to ask when AI systems become collaborators in cognitive work rather than passive tools, and how this shift changes the way people think, design, and decide together.

Agentic AI: Opportunities and Challenges in Information Management
Naveen Kumar
Associate Professor of MIS · Price College of Business, University of Oklahoma
▶ Watch

Naveen invites participants to look beyond chatbots and focus on agentic AI systems that plan, reason, and act with people inside enterprise information infrastructures.

Ethical AI: What Do We Gain? What Do We Lose?
Rebecca Huskey
University of Oklahoma
▶ Watch

An exploration of the ethical dimensions of AI adoption — what we gain in efficiency and scale, and what we risk losing in autonomy, privacy, and human judgment.

3

Cluster 03Generative AI for Domain Science, Engineering Design & Data to Decisions

Building High-Resolution Storms with Generative AI
Xiaodong Chen
Assistant Professor · School of Civil Engineering & Meteorology, University of Oklahoma
▶ Watch

Xiaodong shows how researchers use Generative AI to study storms at high spatial resolution and explore consequences for flooding, infrastructure safety, and climate resilience.

Guiding Large Language Models through the Engineering Design Process
Jitesh H. Panchal
Professor of Mechanical Engineering · Purdue University
▶ Watch

Jitesh invites participants to move beyond generic prompting and treat large language models as design partners guided by structured domain knowledge and formal representations.

Generative AI: From Data to Decisions
Kaiyan K. Mistree
Co-Founder & COO, pX (propX) · Dubai, UAE
▶ Watch

Kaiyan shows how a founder team in the Gulf region uses Generative AI to turn fragmented real estate data into decision-ready insights for investors, developers, and governments.

Physical AI for Human-Centered Perception and Action
Karthik Ramani
Donald W. Feddersen Distinguished Professor · Purdue University
▶ Watch

Karthik presents Physical AI as a frontier where human embodiment and generative intelligence come together — enabling learning, design, and real-time coaching through voice, gesture, and sketching.

Relationship Intelligence: AI as a Strategic Asset
Rohan Doctor
Founder & CEO, Louisa AI | Former MD, Goldman Sachs
▶ Watch

Rohan invites participants to rethink relationship data as a strategic asset — showing how AI can help organizations uncover hidden networks across email, calendars, CRM systems, and public sources.

All recordings are free. Subscribe to watch every semester's talks.

youtube.com/@GSC_GCOE — new talks added each semester
